page: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the t2d server family. It features 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance is powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 2 physical cores and a dedicated CPU allocation. The CPU architecture includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX), AES and VAES cryptography, and SHA_NI instructions, but lacks AVX-512 and AMX extensions. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong single-core CPU capability, with stress-ng single-core performance ranking in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, falling into the bottom 25% for both stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with read-only large block and write large block speeds categorized in the bottom 25%, and PassMark memory scores in the bottom 10%. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show average performance, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor.

From a workload perspective, the t2d-standard-2 is best suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, basic web serving, and single-threaded processing tasks that benefit from its strong single-core performance. It is less optimal for memory-intensive databases, multi-threaded high-performance computing, or large-scale LLM inference, as evidenced by its weak multi-core and database benchmark results. The instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for dedicated-core workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or high memory bandwidth.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores, it provides a balanced 1:4 resource ratio. The hardware includes 32 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instructions, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the bottom 25%. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight workloads, basic web serving, and single-threaded applications, but is less suited for memory-intensive databases or parallelized computing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-2 is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, offering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-2 features 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, delivering strong single-core performance for general workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core x86 virtual machine offering strong single-threaded performance for balanced, lightweight general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM on AMD EPYC 7B13 hardware
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25%
Includes 32 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instructions
Lacks bundled local storage, GPU acceleration, and public IPv4 addresses
Best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded tasks, and basic applications
